ud8.1:1.2: at one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's park.
ud8.1:1.3: Now at that time, the *Bhagavā* was instructing, encouraging, rousing, and gladdening the *bhikkhus* with a *dhamma* talk connected with *nibbāna* [*nibbānapaṭisaṁyuttāya*].
ud8.1:1.4: There those *bhikkhus*, having made it their concern, having attended to it, having gathered all their mind to it, with ears inclined, were listening to the *dhamma*.
ud8.1:2.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having understood this matter, on that occasion uttered this inspired utterance:
ud8.1:3.1: “There is, *bhikkhus*, that dimension [*tadāyatanaṁ*], where there is neither earth, nor water, nor fire, nor air; neither the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], nor the dimension of infinite *viññāṇa* [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], nor the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*], nor the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*]; neither this world, nor the other world, nor both moon and sun.
ud8.1:3.2: There, *bhikkhus*, I speak of neither coming, nor going, nor standing, nor passing away, nor arising;
ud8.1:3.3: it is without support, without occurrence, without object.
